home.tagline:
A quiet study of scale —
from the dust on a leaf to the dust between stars.
home.quote: I make pictures slowly. I look until something looks back.

paragraphs: I make pictures slowly. I'd rather sit with a place for an hour than walk past ten of them — most of what I find good seems to ask for that kind of time.
The work moves between scales — pollen on a stamen, an alley at dusk, a galaxy edge-on. To me they're the same picture, asked at different magnifications.
I shoot 35mm digital and a small Pentax 645. I print on warm-tone paper. I do not retouch faces or edit out the small ugly things; they belong to the picture.
Currently based between Tel Aviv and the Galilee. Available for private commissions, prints, and the occasional gallery correspondence — please write.
behindTheLens.pullQuote: The work moves between scales — pollen on a stamen, an alley at dusk, a galaxy edge-on. To me they're the same picture, asked at different magnifications.

contact.heading: Write to me.
contact.body: I read everything. Responses sometimes take a few days — I prefer to write thoughtfully when I can.
